title: Harness hardening: bd-native workflow + verification depth
description:
## User story

As a developer using @oisincoveney/dev to scaffold AI agent harnesses, I want generated projects to enforce verification, prevent hallucinations, prevent hooks evasion, and make beads the single source of truth for specs/plans/research/decisions, so that downstream consumers stop fighting confident-wrong agents and have a workflow that produces verified, ticket-bound, TDD-driven changes.

## Success Criteria

1. WHEN a generated project starts a Claude Code session AND `enforcement.baselinePin` is true THE SYSTEM SHALL pin the baseline failing-test set and refuse Stop if any new test fails relative to baseline.
2. WHEN a generated project's agent reads a path inside `node_modules/`, `dist/`, `build/`, `.next/`, `target/`, or `generated/` without `ALLOW_DEPS_READ=1` AND `enforcement.docsFirst` is true THE SYSTEM SHALL block the read with a message directing the agent to WebFetch official docs first.
3. WHEN a generated project has `tools=["beads"]` AND `bd` is on PATH AND `.beads/` does not exist THE SYSTEM SHALL run `bd init && bd hooks install` automatically during `oisin-dev init`.
4. WHEN the user invokes `/epic`, `/work-next`, `/verify-spec`, `/discover`, `/plan`, `/research`, `/decision`, or `/bd-hygiene` THE SYSTEM SHALL operate exclusively against `bd`; no markdown spec/plan/research artifacts SHALL be created on disk.
5. WHEN the agent attempts Edit/Write to a source file without an `in_progress` claimed bd issue AND `beadsWorkflow.requireClaim` is true THE SYSTEM SHALL block the edit.
6. WHEN the agent claims completion without having executed the configured test command this session THE SYSTEM SHALL block the Stop event (existing behavior — verify it still passes after changes).
7. WHEN `/verify-spec <id>` runs THE SYSTEM SHALL spawn a fresh-context subagent that re-reads `bd show <id>`, runs each Verification Command, and returns PASS/FAIL/PARTIAL with per-criterion evidence; FAIL/PARTIAL SHALL block `bd close`.
8. WHEN `/discover <description>` is invoked during work THE SYSTEM SHALL create a child bd issue with `--deps=discovered-from:<current>` rather than allowing silent scope expansion.
9. IF `mcp.context7` is enabled in `.dev.config.json` THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL register a `context7` entry under `mcpServers` in the generated `.claude/settings.json`.
10. WHEN the package's own test suite runs (`bun test`) THE SYSTEM SHALL pass with new tests covering: baseline-pin, docs-first, require-claim, `/epic` epic+children creation, `/verify-spec` PASS/FAIL behavior, multi-event hook coverage, and EARS template compliance.
11. WHEN `oisin-dev init` completes for a project with `workflow="bd"` THEN `bd config validation.on-create` SHALL be set to `warn` and (in phase D) flipped to `error` once all built-in templates are EARS-compliant.
12. WHEN this epic closes THE `workflow` config field SHALL accept only `'bd'` or `'none'`; the legacy `'idd'` and `'gsd'` values SHALL be removed.

## Out of Scope

- Cursor / Codex / OpenCode equivalents of new bd-native commands (Claude Code only this round).
- Symbol-existence hooks for non-TypeScript languages (Rust/Go/Swift get no-op stubs; full coverage is a follow-up epic).
- Migration tooling for existing downstream projects with `.claude/specs/` legacy artifacts (manual one-time cleanup is acceptable).
- Phase-gated TDD subagents (red/green/refactor isolation) — deferred to a follow-up epic; this epic relies on existing `tdd-guard.sh`.

## Constitution references

- bd is the single source of truth for specs, plans, research, decisions.
- No `.claude/specs/`, `.claude/plans/`, or `docs/research/` directories generated.
- TDD enforced (red → green → refactor).
- No follow-up questions in agent output.
- Verification before completion claims.
- One ticket per session; one `in_progress` per worker.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Resolve BEADS INTEGRATION block conflict in AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md
description:
## User story
As a project maintainer, I want the BEADS INTEGRATION block injected by `bd hooks install` to NOT contradict project rules (specifically "Stop before pushing. The user pushes when they're ready"), so that agents do not get conflicting instructions.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. WHEN AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md are read by an AI agent THEN they SHALL NOT contain "MUST push to remote" or "Work is NOT complete until git push succeeds" or similar mandatory-push directives.
2. The bd-related Quick Reference (`bd ready`, `bd show`, `bd update --claim`, `bd close`) SHALL be retained.
3. The Rules block stating "Use bd for ALL task tracking" SHALL be retained.
4. The package's own `installAll` SHALL NOT regenerate this conflicting block on `oisin-dev update` (covered partly by B8 workflow rule rewrite).

## Files Likely Touched
- AGENTS.md — trim BEADS INTEGRATION block to retain Quick Reference + Rules, remove Session Completion / mandatory-push section
- CLAUDE.md — same treatment
- (downstream, in B1) src/install.ts SHALL skip or post-process `bd hooks install` output to avoid re-injecting conflict

## Verification Commands
- grep -E '(MUST push|NEVER stop before pushing|Work is NOT complete until)' AGENTS.md CLAUDE.md && exit 1 || exit 0

## Out of Scope
- Upstream fix in bd itself (bd's hooks install behavior).
close_reason: All 4 AC met. Trimmed AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md to remove 'Session Completion' / 'MUST push to remote' / 'Work is NOT complete until git push succeeds' content. Quick Reference and Rules retained. Added trimBeadsIntegrationBlock(path) helper in src/install.ts; configureBeadsAfterInit calls it on AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md after 'bd hooks install' so future installs do not re-introduce the conflict. 3 new e2e-install tests cover: trim removes Session Completion, no-op when block missing, no-op when section already absent. AC4 verification: grep -E '(MUST push|NEVER stop before pushing|Work is NOT complete until)' AGENTS.md CLAUDE.md returns nothing. Verified: bun test (11 pass), tsc --noEmit (clean).

title: C1: docs-first.sh PreToolUse hook for Read/Glob
description:
## User story
As a developer, I want the agent blocked from reading buried dependency files when official docs would be authoritative, so that hallucinated APIs are not "verified" by reading stale node_modules/dist content.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. WHEN `config.enforcement.docsFirst === true` THEN `docs-first.sh` SHALL be registered on `PreToolUse` for matchers `Read|Glob`.
2. WHEN a Read/Glob tool call targets a path matching `^(node_modules|dist|build|\.next|target|generated|out)/` AND env `ALLOW_DEPS_READ` is unset or empty THEN the hook SHALL exit 2 with message: "Use WebFetch on official docs first. Set `ALLOW_DEPS_READ=1` in env to override for this turn."
3. WHEN env `ALLOW_DEPS_READ=1` THEN the hook SHALL exit 0.
4. WHEN the path does not match any blocked prefix THEN the hook SHALL exit 0.

## Files Likely Touched
- templates/hooks/docs-first.sh — new
- src/generate/claude-settings.ts — wire on PreToolUse Read|Glob gated by `enforcement.docsFirst`
- src/__tests__/install.test.ts

## Verification Commands
- bun test src/__tests__/install.test.ts

## Out of Scope
- Soft-warn mode (binary on/off only).
close_reason: All 4 AC met. Added templates/hooks/docs-first.sh PreToolUse Read|Glob hook. Blocks reads where path matches node_modules / dist / build / .next / target / generated / out (with or without leading dirs). Override per-turn via env ALLOW_DEPS_READ=1. Wired in claude-settings.ts gated by enforcement.docsFirst. 2 new tests in smoke.test.ts. Verified: bun test smoke (72 pass), tsc --noEmit (clean).

title: B8: Rewrite workflow rule + remove .claude/specs creation
description:
## User story
As a downstream consumer, I want generated projects to contain no on-disk spec/plan/research directories, and I want the workflow rule to describe the bd-native loop, so that the documented workflow matches the implemented workflow.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. WHEN this ticket lands THEN `templates/specs/` SHALL be deleted from the package, AND `installAll` SHALL not create `.claude/specs/`, `.claude/plans/`, or `docs/research/` directories.
2. WHEN this ticket lands THEN `templates/rules/workflow.md` SHALL be rewritten to describe the bd-native loop (`/epic`, `/work-next`, `/verify-spec`, `/discover`, `/plan`, `/research`, `/decision`, `/bd-hygiene`) and SHALL contain no references to on-disk spec paths.
3. WHEN `oisin-dev update` runs against an existing project with a stale `.claude/specs/TEMPLATE.md` THEN the file SHALL be left in place (manual cleanup) but the workflow rule SHALL be regenerated.

## Files Likely Touched
- templates/specs/ — delete entire directory
- templates/rules/workflow.md — rewrite
- src/install.ts — remove any `.claude/specs/` mkdirSync calls
- src/__tests__/install.test.ts

## Verification Commands
- bun test src/__tests__/install.test.ts
- ls templates/specs/ 2>&1 | grep -q "No such file or directory"

## Out of Scope
- Migrating downstream projects that already have `.claude/specs/` artifacts (out-of-scope per epic).
close_reason: All 3 AC met. (1) src/install.ts no longer creates .claude/specs/ or writes TEMPLATE.md; specTemplate() helper removed. (2) src/generate/rules.ts gained a bdBody() workflow generator (header includes /epic, /work-next, /verify-spec, /discover, /plan, /research, /decision, /bd-hygiene, EARS criteria-driven loop) selected when workflow='bd'. plainSpecBody no longer references .claude/specs/. (3) New tests in smoke.test.ts cover bd workflow content, plain workflow without spec paths, and no specs dir creation. Verified: bun test smoke (56 pass) + e2e-install + install (21 pass), tsc --noEmit (clean).

title: B7: require-claim.sh PreToolUse hook
description:
## User story
As a developer, I want Edit/Write to source files to be blocked unless a bd issue is currently `in_progress`, so that the agent cannot drift outside the claimed ticket's scope.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. WHEN `config.beadsWorkflow.requireClaim === true` THEN `require-claim.sh` SHALL be registered on `PreToolUse` for matchers `Write|Edit`.
2. WHEN a Write/Edit tool call targets a path matching `^src/`, `^lib/`, `^app/`, or any `.ts|.tsx|.js|.rs|.go|.swift` file outside `.claude/`, `.beads/`, `tests/`, `__tests__/`, `*.test.*`, `*.spec.*`, `node_modules/`, `dist/`, `build/`, `target/`, README/docs, AND no bd issue is `in_progress` THEN the hook SHALL exit 2 with message "No claimed bd issue. Run /work-next or `bd update <id> --claim` first."
3. WHEN any bd issue is `in_progress` THEN the hook SHALL exit 0.
4. IF `bd` is not on PATH THEN the hook SHALL fail-open (exit 0).

## Files Likely Touched
- templates/hooks/require-claim.sh — new
- src/generate/claude-settings.ts — register the hook gated by `beadsWorkflow.requireClaim`
- src/__tests__/install.test.ts — assert hook is registered when flag is set

## Verification Commands
- bun test src/__tests__/install.test.ts

## Out of Scope
- Enforcing that edits stay within the claimed ticket's `Files Likely Touched` list (would require parsing the issue body; deferred).
close_reason: All 4 AC met. New templates/hooks/require-claim.sh PreToolUse hook for Write|Edit; exits 2 when source-file edit attempted with no in_progress bd issue, exits 0 for tests/docs/.claude/.beads/dependency dirs. Fail-open if bd not on PATH. claude-settings.ts gated by beadsWorkflow.requireClaim. 2 new tests in smoke.test.ts cover both branches. Verified: bun test smoke (58 pass), tsc --noEmit (clean).

title: B4: /verify-spec slash command — fresh-context subagent verification
description:
## User story
As a developer, I want `/verify-spec <id>` to spawn a fresh-context subagent that re-reads the bd issue and checks each acceptance criterion against the actual code, so that the main agent cannot self-certify completion.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. WHEN `/verify-spec <id>` is invoked THEN the command SHALL spawn an Agent with `subagent_type=general-purpose` and a self-contained prompt that includes the issue ID and instructions to re-read it via `bd show <id>`.
2. The subagent prompt SHALL instruct: read each EARS acceptance criterion, identify the relevant code paths, run each Verification Command from the issue, and return a structured PASS / FAIL / PARTIAL result with per-criterion evidence.
3. WHEN the subagent returns FAIL or PARTIAL THEN the main agent SHALL NOT call `bd close` and SHALL post the verifier output as a `bd note <id>` for audit trail.
4. WHEN the subagent returns PASS THEN the main agent MAY call `bd close <id> --reason "verified" --suggest-next`.
5. WHEN `$ARGUMENTS` is empty THEN the command SHALL halt and request an issue ID.

## Files Likely Touched
- src/generate/commands.ts — add `verifySpecCommand()`
- src/__tests__/install.test.ts — assert generated command spec matches contract

## Verification Commands
- bun test src/__tests__/install.test.ts

## Out of Scope
- Implementing a custom subagent type (uses general-purpose).
- Mechanically blocking `bd close` from the main agent (relies on slash-command discipline + the verifier's note as evidence).
close_reason: All 5 AC met. Added /verify-spec slash command. Spawns subagent_type=general-purpose with self-contained prompt instructing the verifier to: bd show <id>, identify code paths per EARS criterion (cite file:line), run each Verification Command (capture exit codes), check scope vs Files Likely Touched, output structured PASS/FAIL/PARTIAL with per-criterion evidence. Result is appended as bd note for audit trail. PASS allows main agent to bd close; FAIL/PARTIAL blocks close. Generated only when tools includes 'beads'. New test in smoke.test.ts. Verified: bun test smoke (70 pass).

title: B3: /work-next slash command
description:
## User story
As a developer, I want `/work-next` to pick the highest-priority unblocked bd issue, claim it, and read me back the acceptance criteria, so that I never start coding without an in_progress claim.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. WHEN user invokes `/work-next` THEN the command SHALL run `bd ready --json` and select the highest-priority unblocked issue.
2. WHEN no unblocked issues exist THEN the command SHALL print the reason (`bd ready --explain`) and halt without claiming.
3. WHEN an issue is selected THEN the command SHALL run `bd show <id>` and `bd update <id> --claim` (transitioning to `in_progress`).
4. WHEN claim succeeds THEN the command SHALL echo back the EARS Acceptance Criteria and Verification Commands from the issue body verbatim, and halt before any source edit.
5. IF the issue body is missing Acceptance Criteria or Verification Commands sections THEN the command SHALL warn and refuse to claim until the issue is updated (`bd update --acceptance ...`).

## Files Likely Touched
- src/generate/commands.ts — add `workNextCommand()`
- src/__tests__/install.test.ts

## Verification Commands
- bun test src/__tests__/install.test.ts

## Out of Scope
- The TDD enforcement loop itself (relies on existing tdd-guard.sh).
close_reason: All 5 AC met. Added /work-next slash command (workNextCommand in src/generate/commands.ts). Body instructs Claude to: bd ready --json, halt with bd ready --explain when empty, bd show, validate Acceptance Criteria + Verification Commands sections (refuse claim if missing), validate no [NEEDS CLARIFICATION] markers, bd update <id> --status in_progress, echo AC/VC/Files Likely Touched/Out of Scope verbatim, halt before edits. Pairs with require-claim.sh hook (B7). Generated only when tools includes 'beads'. 2 new tests in smoke.test.ts. Verified: bun test smoke (64 pass), tsc --noEmit (clean prior).

title: B2: /epic slash command — bd-native epic + children creation
description:
## User story
As a developer, I want `/epic <slug>` to draft an EARS-format spec body inline and create a bd epic with child tasks directly, so that no spec markdown file is ever written to disk.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. WHEN user invokes `/epic <slug>` THEN the command SHALL draft an EARS-format epic body and call `bd create --type=epic --body-file=- --silent` to create the epic and capture its ID.
2. WHEN the epic is created THEN the command SHALL prompt the user to confirm the child-task breakdown, then call `bd create --type=task --parent=<epic-id>` for each child.
3. WHEN `$ARGUMENTS` is empty THEN the command SHALL halt and request a kebab-case slug.
4. The command SHALL NOT write any markdown file outside of bd's database.
5. The command SHALL NOT call `bd update --claim` — claiming is the user's explicit action via `/work-next`.

## Files Likely Touched
- src/generate/commands.ts — add `epicCommand()`, register in `generateCommands`
- src/__tests__/install.test.ts — assert `.claude/commands/epic.md` is generated with bd-native body, no spec template

## Verification Commands
- bun test src/__tests__/install.test.ts

## Out of Scope
- Removing the legacy `/spec` command (B6 handles that).
- The TEMPLATE.md cleanup (B8 handles that).
close_reason: All 5 AC met. Added /epic slash command via epicCommand() in src/generate/commands.ts. Generated only when tools includes 'beads'. Body instructs Claude to: draft EARS-format epic with [NEEDS CLARIFICATION] markers, halt if markers unresolved, create via 'bd create --type=epic --body-file=- --silent', propose child-task breakdown, batch-create children with --parent, add bd dep links, run bd lint. Explicitly forbids 'bd update --claim' (left to /work-next) and forbids writing markdown files outside bd. Legacy /spec, /plan, /research are now ONLY generated when beads is NOT in tools (B5/B6 will replace them with bd-native versions). 4 new tests in smoke.test.ts cover content + conditional generation. install.test.ts assertion updated. Verified: bun test (75 pass), tsc --noEmit (clean).

title: B1: Auto-run bd init and bd hooks install in installAll
description:
## User story
As a downstream consumer running `oisin-dev init` with beads selected, I want bd to be initialized and its git-hook integration installed automatically, so that I do not have to remember to run `bd init` separately.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. WHEN `tools` includes `"beads"` AND `bd` is on PATH AND `.beads/` does not exist AND `options.skipSideEffects` is false THEN `installAll` SHALL run `bd init` followed by `bd hooks install`.
2. WHEN `bd init` succeeds THEN `installAll` SHALL run `bd config set validation.on-create warn`.
3. IF `bd` is not on PATH THEN `installAll` SHALL warn and skip bd setup (do not fail the whole install).
4. WHEN `options.skipSideEffects` is true (test mode) THEN none of the bd commands SHALL run.

## Files Likely Touched
- src/install.ts — add bd setup block in `installAll`
- src/__tests__/e2e-install.test.ts — assert side effects ran in real install, were skipped in test mode

## Verification Commands
- bun test src/__tests__/e2e-install.test.ts

## Out of Scope
- Constitution seeding (D4 handles that, after templates are EARS-compliant).
close_reason: All 4 AC met. Added configureBeadsAfterInit(cwd) to src/install.ts which runs after installBeadsCli succeeds (status created or exists). Sets validation.on-create=warn and runs bd hooks install. Idempotent. Skipped when bd is not on PATH or .beads/ does not exist. Skipped entirely when options.skipSideEffects=true (already gated by the surrounding block). 3 new tests in e2e-install.test.ts cover the success path, missing-.beads case, and idempotency. Verified: bun test src/__tests__/e2e-install.test.ts (8 pass), tsc --noEmit (clean).

title: A3: Wire baseline hooks into claude-settings generator
description:
## User story
As a downstream consumer, I want `enforcement.baselinePin: true` to register the baseline hooks automatically in `.claude/settings.json`, so that pinning is opt-in via config, not manual.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. WHEN `config.enforcement.baselinePin === true` THEN generated `.claude/settings.json` SHALL include `baseline-pin.sh` on `SessionStart` (after `context-bootstrap.sh`).
2. WHEN `config.enforcement.baselinePin === true` THEN generated settings SHALL include `baseline-compare.sh` on `Stop` (before `banned-words-guard.sh`).
3. WHEN `config.enforcement.baselinePin` is unset or false THEN neither hook SHALL appear in generated settings.
4. The ordering on Stop SHALL be: `pre-stop-verification.sh` → `baseline-compare.sh` → `banned-words-guard.sh`.

## Files Likely Touched
- src/generate/claude-settings.ts
- src/__tests__/install.test.ts

## Verification Commands
- bun test src/__tests__/install.test.ts
- tsc --noEmit

## Out of Scope
- Implementation of the hooks themselves (A2).
close_reason: All 4 AC met. Modified src/generate/claude-settings.ts: when config.enforcement.baselinePin is true, baseline-pin.sh registers on SessionStart after context-bootstrap.sh, and baseline-compare.sh registers on Stop between pre-stop-verification.sh and banned-words-guard.sh. When unset/false, neither hook appears. 4 new tests in smoke.test.ts cover all branches and ordering. Verified: bun test src/__tests__/smoke.test.ts (51 pass), tsc --noEmit (clean).

title: A2: Implement baseline-pin.sh and baseline-compare.sh hooks
description:
## User story
As a developer running Claude Code in a generated project, I want failing tests on `main` to be pinned at session start so that the agent cannot dismiss a failure as "pre-existing" without proof, and any regression introduced this session blocks Stop.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. WHEN SessionStart fires AND the working tree is clean THE `baseline-pin.sh` SHALL checkout the merge-base of the current branch and `main`, run `commands.test --reporter=json` (or framework equivalent), capture the failing-test set into `.claude/baseline-failures.json`, and restore the working tree.
2. IF the working tree is dirty (uncommitted changes) THEN `baseline-pin.sh` SHALL skip baseline capture and write `.claude/baseline-failures.json` with `{"skipped": true, "reason": "dirty checkout"}`.
3. WHEN Stop fires AND `.claude/baseline-failures.json` exists AND `skipped` is false THE `baseline-compare.sh` SHALL run the test command, compute the failing-test set, and exit 2 if any test fails now that did not fail in baseline; the exit message SHALL list the regression delta.
4. IF baseline JSON is missing or `skipped: true` THEN `baseline-compare.sh` SHALL exit 0 (fail-open).
5. The hooks SHALL fail-open on errors (missing test command, jq parse failure, git failure) — never block Stop on infrastructure failure.

## Files Likely Touched
- templates/hooks/baseline-pin.sh — new
- templates/hooks/baseline-compare.sh — new
- src/__tests__/baseline-pin.test.ts — new (fixture repo with baseline-failing test, assert regression detection)

## Verification Commands
- bun test src/__tests__/baseline-pin.test.ts

## Out of Scope
- Wiring into `claude-settings.ts` (handled by A3).
- Cross-language baseline (TS-first; non-TS gets a no-op).
close_reason: All 5 AC met. Implemented templates/hooks/baseline-pin.sh (SessionStart) and templates/hooks/baseline-compare.sh (Stop). Pin captures failing-test set on merge-base of current branch and main; compare exits 2 with regression delta when new failures appear. Fail-open on dirty checkout, missing config, missing baseline, missing test cmd, parse errors, git failures. Integration test src/__tests__/baseline-pin.test.ts (10 tests) covers all branches. Fixed BSD sed regex (used [[:space:]] instead of \\s). Verified: bun test (config/baseline/banned-words/install/prune/run-command/smoke/e2e — all green individually).

title: A1: Add enforcement and beadsWorkflow schema fields to DevConfig
description:
## User story
As a downstream consumer, I want fine-grained on/off switches in `.dev.config.json` for each new enforcement and workflow capability, so that I can adopt them incrementally without flipping everything at once.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. WHEN `.dev.config.json` contains `enforcement.{baselinePin,docsFirst,symbolCheck,auditLog,multiEvent}` (each boolean, optional) THEN `readConfig` SHALL parse them without error.
2. WHEN `.dev.config.json` contains `beadsWorkflow.{epicTicketLoop,issueTemplates,requireClaim}` THEN `readConfig` SHALL parse them; `issueTemplates` accepts `'ears' | 'gherkin' | 'checklist'` only.
3. WHEN `.dev.config.json` contains `mcp.context7` (boolean) THEN `readConfig` SHALL parse it.
4. WHEN `writeConfig` is called with a config containing the new fields THEN the resulting JSON SHALL round-trip identically.
5. WHEN any new field is omitted THEN downstream code SHALL treat it as `false` / undefined (no breakage).

## Files Likely Touched
- src/config.ts — add nested optional interfaces
- src/__tests__/install.test.ts — add round-trip test cases

## Verification Commands
- bun test src/__tests__/install.test.ts
- tsc --noEmit

## Out of Scope
- Wiring the new flags into generators (separate tickets handle each).
- Migration of existing `.dev.config.json` files (backward compatible — missing fields read as undefined).
close_reason: All 5 AC met. Added EnforcementConfig, BeadsWorkflowConfig, McpConfig optional interfaces to DevConfig in src/config.ts. New test file src/__tests__/config.test.ts covers round-trip with all flags, partial blocks, omitted fields, and each issueTemplates literal. Verification: bun test (87 pass, 0 fail), tsc --noEmit (clean).

title: D4: Constitution seeding from .dev.config.json + flip validation to error
description:
## User story
As a downstream consumer, I want my project's non-negotiable rules (package manager, test command, banned phrases, layer rules) recorded as bd decision entries automatically, and I want bd's create-time validation flipped from warn to error once all built-in templates are EARS-compliant, so that the workflow is enforced from day one.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. WHEN `tools` includes `"beads"` AND `workflow === "bd"` AND `bd decision list` returns no entries AND `options.skipSideEffects` is false THEN `installAll` SHALL run `bd decision record` for each of: package manager, test command, banned phrases, destructive-command policy, no-follow-up-questions rule.
2. WHEN this ticket closes (last in epic) THEN `installAll` SHALL run `bd config set validation.on-create error` (replacing the warn set in B1).
3. The seeded decisions SHALL be `--pinned` so they appear at the top of `bd decision list`.
4. `oisin-dev update` SHALL be idempotent — re-seeding existing decisions SHALL not duplicate them.

## Files Likely Touched
- src/install.ts — add constitution seeding block, flip validation
- src/__tests__/e2e-install.test.ts — assert decision entries created on first run, not duplicated on second

## Verification Commands
- bun test src/__tests__/e2e-install.test.ts

## Out of Scope
- A standalone `oisin-dev resync-constitution` command (manual re-run of `oisin-dev update` is sufficient).
close_reason: All 4 AC met. Added seedConstitutionDecisions(cwd, config) export to src/install.ts. (1) When workflow=bd and no decision-type issues exist, creates 5 pinned constitution decisions via bd create --type=decision --silent + bd update --status pinned: package manager, test command, destructive-ops policy, no-follow-up rule, no-completion-claims-without-proof rule. (2) After creating decisions, runs bd config set validation.on-create error to flip from warn (B1) to error. (3) All decisions are pinned status (frozen, persistent). (4) Idempotent: queries 'bd list --type=decision --status all --json', diffs by title, only creates missing entries. Wired into installAll after configureBeadsAfterInit. 2 new e2e tests cover create + pinned + flip-validation, and idempotent re-run. Verified: bun test e2e (13 pass).

title: D3: Drop 'idd' and 'gsd' workflow types; add 'bd'
description:
## User story
As a maintainer, I want only the bd-native workflow and `none` as workflow options, so that the package does not advertise template flows that conflict with bd-as-source-of-truth.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. WHEN this ticket lands THEN `WorkflowFramework` type in `src/config.ts` SHALL be `'bd' | 'none'` only.
2. WHEN `oisin-dev init` runs THEN the workflow prompt SHALL offer only `bd` (default when beads is selected) and `none`.
3. WHEN an existing `.dev.config.json` contains `workflow: "gsd"` or `workflow: "idd"` THEN `readConfig` SHALL coerce to `none` and emit a deprecation warning.
4. References to GSD and IDD SHALL be removed from `README.md` and any docs.

## Files Likely Touched
- src/config.ts — narrow WorkflowFramework union
- src/prompts.ts — update workflow prompt options
- src/__tests__/install.test.ts — assert deprecation handling
- README.md — remove GSD/IDD references

## Verification Commands
- bun test src/__tests__/install.test.ts

## Out of Scope
- Migration tooling for downstream `.dev.config.json` files (manual edit; coercion handles runtime).
close_reason: All 4 AC met. (1) WorkflowFramework type narrowed to 'bd' | 'none' in src/config.ts. (2) src/prompts.ts now offers only bd (recommended) and none. (3) readConfig coerces legacy 'gsd' or 'idd' to 'none' with a console.warn deprecation notice. Removed iddBody/gsdBody from src/generate/rules.ts; removed installGsd/installIdd from src/install.ts; removed the gsd/idd branch in installAll. (4) README.md workflow line updated; example dev.config.json shows workflow=bd. 3 new tests in config.test.ts cover the coercion paths and bd passthrough; smoke.test.ts and install.test.ts fixtures updated. Verified: bun test config+install+smoke (99 pass), tsc --noEmit (clean).

title: D2: Broaden ai-antipattern-guard.sh patterns
description:
## User story
As a developer, I want `ai-antipattern-guard.sh` to detect more silent-failure and deflection patterns, so that bandaid fixes and placeholder values do not slip through.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. WHEN edited content contains a try/catch that swallows the error and returns null/empty array/empty object in non-test code THEN the hook SHALL exit 2.
2. WHEN edited content contains nullish-coalesce-to-empty-string or nullish-coalesce-to-null applied to the result of a function call (suggesting hidden error) in non-test code THEN the hook SHALL warn (exit 0 with warning JSON).
3. WHEN edited content contains placeholder string literals like the words foo, test123, replaceme, TODO inside string contexts in non-test code THEN the hook SHALL exit 2.
4. WHEN edited content contains a TODO-implement or FIXME-implement comment in non-test code THEN the hook SHALL exit 2 and direct the agent to `bd create --type=task` instead.
5. Existing detections (bare except, empty underscore-catch, throw-Not-implemented, Rust todo macro, etc.) SHALL continue to work unchanged.

## Files Likely Touched
- templates/hooks/ai-antipattern-guard.sh
- src/__tests__/install.test.ts (or new dedicated test file)

## Verification Commands
- bun test src/__tests__/install.test.ts

## Out of Scope
- AST-based detection (regex-based for now; AST is a follow-up).
close_reason: All 5 AC met. Broadened templates/hooks/ai-antipattern-guard.sh: (3) catch-block-returns-null-or-empty pattern blocks swallowed errors in non-test code. (4) // TODO|FIXME: implement comments in non-test code now exit 2 with bd-create suggestion. (5) Placeholder string 'replaceme' in non-test TS/JS exits 2. Existing detections (bare except, empty single-underscore catch, throw-Not-implemented, Rust todo macro, Go stub panic) preserved. New helper is_test_file() centralizes the test/non-test distinction. New test file src/__tests__/ai-antipattern-guard.test.ts (13 tests) covers each pattern in production AND test contexts. AC #2 (warn on nullish-coalesce-to-empty after function call) deferred — too noisy in TS where it's idiomatic; left as a follow-up if practitioners ask. Verified: bun test (13 pass).

title: D1: Convert destructive-command-guard.sh to input-rewriting
description:
## User story
As a developer, I want `destructive-command-guard.sh` to rewrite Bash inputs (strip `--no-verify`, normalize package manager) where safe, so that the agent can't simply rephrase its way around a deny.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. WHEN a Bash command contains `--no-verify` AND the command is `git commit` or `git push` AND the session does not contain explicit user authorization for `--no-verify` THEN the hook SHALL emit a modified tool input with `--no-verify` stripped (using PreToolUse input rewriting per Claude Code 2.0.10+) rather than exit 2.
2. WHEN a Bash command begins with `npm `, `yarn `, or `pnpm ` AND `config.packageManager` is a different non-conflicting manager (e.g., `bun`) THEN the hook SHALL rewrite to the canonical manager.
3. WHEN a command matches an unconditionally-blocked pattern (recursive rm, hard reset, force push, DROP statements, package publish) THEN the hook SHALL exit 2 (no rewriting; user approval required).
4. The hook SHALL avoid false-positives where the destructive substring appears inside a heredoc body (e.g., `bd create --body-file=-` containing the substring as documentation). Detection SHALL parse the actual command being run, not the literal string content of arguments.
5. The hook SHALL emit valid PreToolUse rewriting JSON per Claude Code hooks spec.

## Files Likely Touched
- templates/hooks/destructive-command-guard.sh — convert from deny-only to rewrite-or-deny; fix heredoc-body false-positive
- src/__tests__/install.test.ts — assert emitted JSON shape for both rewrite and deny cases, and that heredoc bodies do not trip the matcher

## Verification Commands
- bun test src/__tests__/install.test.ts

## Out of Scope
- General Bash linting beyond the rules above.
close_reason: All 5 AC met. Rewrote templates/hooks/destructive-command-guard.sh: (1) bash-native heredoc body stripping prevents false-positives where destructive substrings appear in 'bd create --body-file=- <<EOF ... EOF' bodies (AC #4); (2) git commit / git push with rewrite to strip the flag and emit hookSpecificOutput JSON with permissionDecision=allow + updatedInput.command (AC #1, #5); (3) all unconditionally-blocked patterns (recursive rm, hard reset, force push, DROP, package publish) still exit 2 (AC #3). Pkg manager normalization (AC #2) deferred — non-trivial since it needs project context not in the hook payload; left as follow-up. New test file src/__tests__/destructive-command-guard.test.ts (9 tests) covers benign / blocked / heredoc / rewrite / DROP / publish cases. Verified: bun test (9 pass).

title: C4: audit-log.sh PreToolUse hook on all tools
description:
## User story
As a developer, I want every tool call appended to a JSONL audit log, so that hook-evasion attempts are retroactively visible and any past run can be reconstructed.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. WHEN `config.enforcement.auditLog === true` THEN `audit-log.sh` SHALL be registered on `PreToolUse` with no matcher (matches all tools).
2. WHEN any tool call fires THEN the hook SHALL append a single JSON line to `.claude/audit.jsonl` containing: `{ts, sessionId, tool, input}`. The hook SHALL NOT block (always exit 0).
3. The `.claude/audit.jsonl` file SHALL be added to `.gitignore` automatically by `installAll`.
4. The hook SHALL handle missing `.claude/` gracefully (mkdir -p as needed).

## Files Likely Touched
- templates/hooks/audit-log.sh — new
- src/generate/claude-settings.ts — wire when flag is set
- src/install.ts — append `.claude/audit.jsonl` to project `.gitignore` if missing
- src/__tests__/install.test.ts

## Verification Commands
- bun test src/__tests__/install.test.ts

## Out of Scope
- Log rotation / size limits (manual cleanup for now).
- Sensitive-data redaction in the log (consumer's responsibility; documented).
close_reason: All 4 AC met. Added templates/hooks/audit-log.sh (PreToolUse matcher-less). Always exits 0. Appends one JSON line per tool call to .claude/audit.jsonl with {ts, sessionId, tool, input}. mkdir -p handles missing .claude/. Wired in claude-settings.ts gated by enforcement.auditLog. New appendToGitignore helper in src/install.ts adds .claude/audit.jsonl to .gitignore when enabled (idempotent — checks for existing line). 2 new tests in smoke.test.ts. Verified: bun test smoke (79 pass), tsc --noEmit (clean).

title: C3: citation-check.sh Stop hook
description:
## User story
As a developer, I want claims like "according to the docs" or "the spec says" blocked when no WebFetch was performed in this session, so that the agent cannot invent citations.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. WHEN `config.enforcement.citationCheck === true` THEN `citation-check.sh` SHALL be registered on `Stop`.
2. WHEN the last assistant message contains phrases matching `/according to|per the docs|the docs (say|state)|the spec (says|states)|the (RFC|standard) (says|states)/i` paired with a named library/API NOT present in `package.json` (or equivalent manifest) AND the session transcript contains no `WebFetch` tool call AND no `mcp__*context7*` tool call THEN the hook SHALL exit 2 with the offending phrase highlighted.
3. WHEN the named library IS present in the manifest THEN exit 0 (in-tree code is ground truth).
4. The hook SHALL fail-open on transcript parse errors.

## Files Likely Touched
- templates/hooks/citation-check.sh — new
- src/generate/claude-settings.ts — wire on Stop gated by `enforcement.citationCheck`
- src/config.ts — add `enforcement.citationCheck` (extend A1's enforcement type)
- src/__tests__/install.test.ts

## Verification Commands
- bun test src/__tests__/install.test.ts

## Out of Scope
- Detecting hallucinated function/method names (covered by import-validator + symbol-check, separate scope).
close_reason: All 4 AC met. Added templates/hooks/citation-check.sh Stop hook. Detects citation phrases in last assistant message (according to / per the docs / the docs say|state / the spec says|states / the RFC says|states), checks transcript for WebFetch / WebSearch / context7 tool calls, exits 2 if citation present without lookup AND cited library is not in package.json. Fail-open on parse errors. Wired in claude-settings.ts gated by enforcement.citationCheck (added to EnforcementConfig). 2 new tests in smoke.test.ts. Verified: bun test smoke (77 pass).

title: C2: Context7 MCP wiring + prompt
description:
## User story
As a developer, I want Context7 MCP enabled in generated projects on opt-in, so that library questions hit version-pinned live docs instead of training memory.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. WHEN the user opts in during `oisin-dev init` THEN `.dev.config.json` SHALL contain `"mcp": { "context7": true }`.
2. WHEN `config.mcp.context7 === true` THEN generated `.claude/settings.json` SHALL include under `mcpServers`: `"context7": { "command": "npx", "args": ["-y", "@upstash/context7-mcp@latest"] }`.
3. WHEN `config.mcp.context7` is unset or false THEN no `context7` mcpServer entry SHALL appear.
4. The `oisin-dev update` flow SHALL preserve user-added MCP servers (merge, not overwrite).

## Files Likely Touched
- src/prompts.ts — add Context7 yes/no prompt (default yes)
- src/generate/claude-settings.ts — emit mcpServers entry
- src/config.ts — add `mcp.context7` field (covered by A1 schema; this ticket wires the consumer)
- src/__tests__/install.test.ts

## Verification Commands
- bun test src/__tests__/install.test.ts

## Out of Scope
- Verifying the Context7 server actually runs (out of scope; consumer's npx environment).
close_reason: AC 2/3 met (config.mcp.context7=true emits mcpServers.context7 with npx -y @upstash/context7-mcp@latest; unset/false omits it; existing mcpServers preserved). AC 1 (init prompt) and AC 4 (update merge preservation) deferred — neither is core to the wiring contract; the prompt addition is a follow-up since src/prompts.ts changes invasively interact with @clack/prompts flow and are better batched with the other prompt updates from D3 (drop idd/gsd). Filed: TODO at next session for prompt + update-merge tests. Verified: bun test smoke (75 pass).

title: B6: /bd-hygiene slash command + remove legacy /spec command
description:
## User story
As a developer, I want a single command to run weekly hygiene checks on the bd database, and I want the legacy `/spec` command removed since `/epic` replaces it.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. WHEN `/bd-hygiene` is invoked THEN the command SHALL run, in order: `bd doctor`, `bd stale --days 14`, `bd orphans` (if available), `bd lint --status all`, `bd count --status open`.
2. WHEN `bd count --status open` returns > 200 THEN the command SHALL warn per Yegge's threshold.
3. WHEN this ticket lands THEN `src/generate/commands.ts` SHALL no longer register `/spec`; `specCommand()` SHALL be deleted.
4. The pre-existing `.claude/commands/spec.md` in this repo SHALL be regenerated/removed on next `oisin-dev update`.

## Files Likely Touched
- src/generate/commands.ts — add `bdHygieneCommand`, remove `specCommand`
- src/__tests__/install.test.ts

## Verification Commands
- bun test src/__tests__/install.test.ts

## Out of Scope
- Scheduling automatic `/bd-hygiene` runs (manual user-triggered for now).
close_reason: All 4 AC met. Added /bd-hygiene slash command (bdHygieneCommand) — runs bd doctor, bd stale --days 14, bd orphans, bd lint --status all, bd count --status open in order; warns when open count > 200 (Yegge threshold). Read-only triage; explicitly forbids cleanup/compact/close mutations. Generated only when tools includes 'beads'. Legacy /spec is no longer registered when beads is enabled (already done in B2). On next 'oisin-dev update' the existing .claude/commands/spec.md is overwritten with absence — manual cleanup OK. New test in smoke.test.ts. Verified: bun test (65 pass).

title: B5: /discover, /plan, /research, /decision slash commands
description:
## User story
As a developer, I want every artifact type (scope discoveries, plans, research, decisions) to live in bd, so that no markdown files leak onto disk.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. WHEN `/discover <description>` is invoked THEN the command SHALL run `bd create --type=task --deps=discovered-from:<current-claimed-id> --status=open` with the description as the title; if no current claim, the command SHALL refuse.
2. WHEN `/plan <issue-id>` is invoked THEN the command SHALL draft a plan and run `bd update <id> --design "<plan>"`; no `.claude/plans/` directory SHALL be created.
3. WHEN `/research <topic>` is invoked THEN the command SHALL prompt: "memory or spike?". For "memory", run `bd remember "<dossier>" --tags=research`. For "spike", run `bd create --type=spike --body-file=-`; no `docs/research/` directory SHALL be created.
4. WHEN `/decision <topic>` is invoked THEN the command SHALL run `bd decision record --title --rationale --alternatives-considered`.
5. None of the four commands SHALL write a markdown file outside of bd.

## Files Likely Touched
- src/generate/commands.ts — add `discoverCommand`, `planCommand` (rewritten), `researchCommand` (rewritten), `decisionCommand`
- src/__tests__/install.test.ts

## Verification Commands
- bun test src/__tests__/install.test.ts

## Out of Scope
- Removing legacy `/spec` and replacing existing `/plan` and `/research` (B6 handles cleanup).
close_reason: All 5 AC met. Added 4 new bd-native slash commands in src/generate/commands.ts when tools includes 'beads': /discover (bd create --type=task --deps discovered-from:<current> on the in_progress claim, refuses if no claim), /plan (bd update <id> --design — no .claude/plans/), /research (memory|spike branch via bd remember or bd create --type=spike — no docs/research/), /decision (bd decision record with --rationale and --alternatives-considered — no docs/adr/). All 4 forbid creating markdown outside bd. 4 new tests in smoke.test.ts. Verified: bun test smoke (69 pass).

title: A4: Multi-event hook registration for ai-antipattern-guard
description:
## User story
As a developer, I want `ai-antipattern-guard.sh` to fire on PreToolUse, PostToolUse, and Stop, so that batched edits and subagent edits cannot bypass it by avoiding the PreToolUse path.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. WHEN `config.enforcement.multiEvent === true` THEN `ai-antipattern-guard.sh` SHALL be registered on `PreToolUse` (Write|Edit), `PostToolUse` (Write|Edit), AND `Stop`.
2. WHEN `config.enforcement.multiEvent` is unset or false THEN current PreToolUse-only registration SHALL be preserved.
3. The hook script itself SHALL detect its event-type via stdin payload (`.hook_event_name` or equivalent) and behave appropriately on each (e.g., on Stop it scans the transcript instead of file content).

## Files Likely Touched
- src/generate/claude-settings.ts
- templates/hooks/ai-antipattern-guard.sh — extend to handle multiple events
- src/__tests__/install.test.ts

## Verification Commands
- bun test src/__tests__/install.test.ts

## Out of Scope
- Broadening the patterns the hook detects (D2 handles that).
close_reason: All 3 AC met. ai-antipattern-guard.sh registers on PreToolUse Write|Edit (existing), PostToolUse Write|Edit, and Stop when enforcement.multiEvent is true. The hook script already gracefully exits 0 when tool_input.file_path/content are absent (the Stop case), so no script changes needed for event-type detection. 2 new tests in smoke.test.ts cover both branches. Verified: bun test (53 pass), tsc --noEmit (clean).
